body.title: agent_datacenter: browser-use evaluation spike — answer 3 shim questions
body.result: GO verdict. lab/spikes/browser-use-eval.md: interface stable (pin 0.12.x), needs own shim (BaseShim maps: start=launch Chrome CDP, stop=kill, self_test=navigate), restart=kill+reopen (storage_state opt-in). Unblocks device+shim tickets.
body.description:
Before a browser-use shim is scoped, answer the 3 open questions from the spec.

Questions to answer:
1. Does browser-use have a stable enough interface to shim against? (check current pypi version, changelog stability, breaking changes in last 6 months)
2. Does it need its own shim or can it ride an existing automation shim? (e.g. a generic 'subprocess automation' shim pattern)
3. What does 'restart' mean for a browser session? (kill and reopen? restore session state? reconnect to existing session?)

Proposed shape:
- Spike: install browser-use; read its API; attempt a minimal 'connect, navigate, close' cycle
- Write lab/spikes/browser-use-eval.md: answers to all 3 questions + go/no-go recommendation
- If go: T-adc-browser-use-device and T-adc-browser-use-shim are unblocked
- If no-go: those tickets get DISCARD status with reason documented

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/lab/spikes/browser-use-eval.md (new)

Design rules:
- None apply — spike/research only

Scope boundary:
IN: evaluation, spike attempt, written recommendation
OUT: any production browser-use device code

Test plan:
- lab/spikes/browser-use-eval.md exists with all 3 questions answered
- go/no-go stated explicitly
